name: data-visualization
description: Data visualization with chart selection, color theory, and annotation best practices. Covers chart types (bar, line, scatter, heatmap), axes rules, and storytelling with data. Use for: charts, graphs, dashboards, reports, presentations, infographics, data stories. Triggers: data visualization, chart, graph, data chart, bar chart, line chart, scatter plot, data viz, visualization, dashboard chart, infographic data, data presentation, chart design, plot, heatmap, pie chart alternative
capabilities: Generate matplotlib charts for line, bar, scatter, heatmap, and KPI visualizations
Apply color theory principles with sequential, diverging, and categorical palettes
Create dark mode compatible visualizations with proper contrast
Add annotations and insights to charts to tell compelling data stories
Convert data to optimal chart types based on relationships (time series, comparison, distribution, correlation)
Export charts as high-resolution PNG files for presentations and reports
author: inference-sh-9
securitySummary: All 37 static analysis findings are false positives. The detected 'external_commands' patterns are markdown code blocks (```bash, ```python) containing documentation examples, not executable code. The 'network' findings are legitimate documentation links to the inference.sh service. No malicious intent, prompt injection attempts, or security risks detected.
valueStatement: Transform raw data into clear, compelling visualizations. Learn which chart types work best for your data, apply color theory principles, and tell powerful stories with your charts.
userTitle: Create Data Visualizations with Best Practices
limitations: Requires external inference.sh CLI tool installation and account
Code examples are Python/matplotlib based, not applicable to other visualization libraries
Cannot interactively modify or update existing chart files
Examples focus on static image export, not interactive web-based visualizations
Color palette examples use specific hex codes that may not match brand guidelines

useCases.title: Business reporting dashboards
useCases.description: Create professional charts for monthly revenue reports, user growth metrics, and KPI cards that communicate insights clearly to stakeholders.

useCases.title: Academic research presentations
useCases.description: Generate publication-quality scatter plots, heatmaps, and comparative charts that follow data visualization best practices for research papers.

useCases.title: Marketing infographics
useCases.description: Design compelling visual stories with annotated charts, color-coded comparisons, and insight-focused titles for presentations and social media.

promptTemplates.title: Basic bar chart for categories
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a horizontal bar chart comparing monthly traffic sources (Email, Social, SEO, Paid Ads, Referral, Direct). Highlight the top performer in blue, others in gray. Add value labels and a descriptive title.

promptTemplates.title: Time series line chart
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a line chart showing revenue over 12 months. Plot both current year and previous year as comparison. Include a shaded area between lines, annotations for peak values, and a title that states the growth insight.

promptTemplates.title: KPI card visualization
promptTemplates.prompt: Create three KPI cards showing Monthly Revenue ($89K), Active Users (12.4K), and Churn Rate (2.1%). Include percentage change indicators with color coding (green for positive, red for negative).

promptTemplates.title: Dark mode heatmap
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a heatmap showing website traffic by day and hour. Use a dark theme with white text, blues color scale, and value labels in each cell. Add a colorbar legend and descriptive title.

bestPractices: Always start Y-axis at zero for bar charts to avoid misleading visual comparisons
Use insight-focused titles that state the conclusion (e.g., 'Revenue doubled') rather than generic labels (e.g., 'Revenue Chart')
Limit to 5-7 colors per visualization and use gray to de-emphasize non-critical elements
antiPatterns: Avoid pie charts for data visualization—use horizontal bar charts or treemaps instead for accurate comparisons
Do not use 3D charts as they distort data perception and appear unprofessional
Never sort bar charts alphabetically—always sort by value to reveal the data story

faq.question: What tools does this skill require?
faq.answer: This skill requires the inference.sh CLI tool, which you install via curl script. The CLI executes Python code with matplotlib to generate visualizations.

faq.question: Can I modify the generated charts?
faq.answer: Yes, the Python code examples are fully customizable. You can adjust colors, dimensions, labels, and styling in the matplotlib code before executing.

faq.question: What file formats are supported?
faq.answer: Charts are exported as PNG files at 150 DPI resolution, suitable for presentations, reports, and web use.

faq.question: Do I need Python installed?
faq.answer: No, the Python code runs on the inference.sh platform. You only need their CLI tool to send code execution requests.

faq.question: Can this create interactive charts?
faq.answer: No, this skill focuses on static image generation. For interactive visualizations, consider web-based libraries like Plotly or D3.js.

faq.question: How do I choose the right chart type?
faq.answer: Use line charts for time trends, bar charts for category comparison, scatter plots for correlation, and heatmaps for two-dimensional patterns. The skill includes a comprehensive selection guide.
